title: myosurus minimus var. major
intro: Myosurus minimus var. major, also known as the alpine mouse tail or greater myosurus, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of North America and is characterized by its delicate white flowers and slender stem. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Myosurus minimus var. major include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
